meta_title: For Students: testing automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Build a Portfolio: Master Testing Automation for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle by mastering testing automation. A high-impact guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for intermediate developers.
content.answer_block: To bypass the 'no portfolio' trap, implement robust test suites for your side projects. By demonstrating automated testing proficiency, you shift from a 'code-writer' to a 'systems-engineer' in the eyes of recruiters. Use Antigravity for test generation and Claude Code to automate execution, proving your code is production-ready.
content.intro:
The empty GitHub repository is the silent killer of junior careers. You have the skills, but without a proven track record of reliability, hiring managers see a risk, not an asset. Most students scramble to build 'to-do list' clones, but the market is already flooded with those. The real differentiator isn't the feature set—it's the stability.

By layering professional-grade testing automation over your projects, you transform a fragile script into a resilient software product. This case study shows you how to leverage AI-driven tools to build a portfolio that signals engineering maturity, discipline, and a deep understanding of the software development lifecycle.

steps.title: Define the Test Strategy
steps.description: Identify the critical business logic in your app that requires 100% coverage. Focus on edge cases rather than trivial UI interactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current repository structure and identify the three most critical functions that require unit tests to ensure production stability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze repo structure for test coverage'

steps.title: Generate Test Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to create a robust test suite structure, ensuring it follows industry standards like AAA (Arrange, Act, Assert).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive Pytest suite for the identified functions, including edge cases for null inputs, boundary values, and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate pytest suite for critical functions'

steps.title: Automate Execution with Claude Code
steps.description: Configure Claude Code to act as your continuous integration agent, running tests automatically whenever you commit changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a CI/CD configuration script that integrates with my GitHub Actions to trigger these tests on every push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create github actions workflow for automated testing'

steps.title: Implement Mutation Testing
steps.description: Go beyond simple coverage by using mutation testing to verify the quality of your tests themselves.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain how to integrate mutation testing into my current pipeline to ensure my tests are actually catching logic errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup mutation testing library and run report'

steps.title: Documenting Results for Employers
steps.description: Create a 'Testing Report' section in your README to showcase your technical rigor to recruiters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README section explaining my testing methodology, coverage percentage, and the importance of reliability in my project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate professional testing documentation for readme'

pro_tips: Always test the 'Happy Path' last; focus on failure modes first to prove you understand defensive programming.
Use snapshot testing for UI components to quickly detect regressions without writing manual assertions for every element.
Include a 'Test Coverage' badge in your GitHub README; it provides instant visual proof of quality to recruiters.
Treat your test code with the same refactoring rigor as your production code to prevent 'test rot'.
Simulate production environments in your tests using containerization to catch configuration-based bugs early.
content.next_steps: Once your tests are passing consistently, deploy your project to a public URL and include a link to your CI/CD dashboard in your resume.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your projects go unnoticed by recruiters. Build with confidence, automate your success, and prove your engineering value today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
